steps.title: Count the gaps
steps.content:
The wall length is $4x$ feet, and consecutive clips are spaced $\frac{4}{7}$ feet apart. The number of gaps is
$$\frac{4x}{\frac{4}{7}}=4x\cdot\frac{7}{4}=7x$$

steps.title: Add the first clip
steps.content: A row with $7x$ gaps has one more clip than gaps because there is a clip at both ends. $$y=7x+1$$ The answer is D.
